subsection.heading: To obtain data and inspect income returns

content.p: For powers of the Joint Committee to obtain and inspect income returns, see section 6103(f).

subsection.heading: Relating to hearings and sessions
subsection.chapeau: The Joint Committee, or any subcommittee thereof, is authorized—

content.p: To hold hearings and to sit and act at such places and times;

paragraph.heading: To require attendance of witnesses and production of books

content.p: To require by subpoena (to be issued under the signature of the chairman or vice chairman) or otherwise the attendance of such witnesses and the production of such books, papers, and documents;
The Joint Committee, or any subcommittee thereof, is authorized to have such printing and binding done as it deems advisable.
The Joint Committee, or any subcommittee thereof, is authorized to make such expenditures as it deems advisable.

The Joint Committee shall review all requests (other than requests by the chairman or ranking member of a committee or subcommittee) for investigations of the Internal Revenue Service by the Government Accountability Office, and approve such requests when appropriate, with a view towards eliminating overlapping investigations, ensuring that the Government Accountability Office has the capacity to handle the investigation, and ensuring that investigations focus on areas of primary importance to tax administration.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2018—Subsec. (f). Pub. L. 115–141 struck out subsec. (f) which related to joint reviews of the strategic plans and budget for the Internal Revenue Service.
2005—Subsec. (e). Pub. L. 109–135 substituted “Government Accountability Office” for “General Accounting Office” in two places.
1976—Subsec. (d). Pub. L. 94–455 struck out par. (2) relating to limitation on cost of stenographic services in reporting hearings.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1998 Amendment
note.p:
Pub. L. 105–206, title IV, § 4001(b), July 22, 1998, 112 Stat. 784, provided that: “(1) Subsection (e) of section 8021 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as added by subsection (a) of this section [amending this section], shall apply to requests made after the date of the enactment of this Act [July 22, 1998].
“(2) Subsection (f) of such section shall take effect on the date of the enactment of this Act.”

note.heading: Effective Date of 1988 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 100–647 effective, except as otherwise provided, as if included in the provision of the Tax Reform Act of 1986, Pub. L. 99–514, to which such amendment relates, see section 1019(a) of Pub. L. 100–647, set out as a note under section 1 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1976 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 94–455 effective on first day of first month which begins more than 90 days after Oct. 4, 1976, see section 1907(c) of Pub. L. 94–455, set out as a note under section 8001 of this title.

note.heading: Savings Provision
note.p: For provisions that nothing in amendment by Pub. L. 115–141 be construed to affect treatment of certain transactions occurring, property acquired, or items of income, loss, deduction, or credit taken into account prior to Mar. 23, 2018, for purposes of determining liability for tax for periods ending after Mar. 23, 2018, see section 401(e) of Pub. L. 115–141, set out as a note under section 23 of this title.

note.heading: Time for Joint Review
note.p: Pub. L. 108–311, title III, § 321(c), Oct. 4, 2004, 118 Stat. 1182, provided that the joint review required by section 8021(f)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (26 U.S.C. 8021(f)(2)) to be made before June 1, 2004, was to be treated as timely if made before June 1, 2005.
